note.soap:
S:  
No history or symptoms were provided during the phone encounter. No chief complaint was captured. No allergies reported. No additional symptom information available.

O:  
No physical examination findings available due to phone-only encounter. No vital signs or objective data provided.

A:  
  - Working diagnosis (most likely):  
    Unable to determine working diagnosis due to lack of symptom or history data.  

  - Differential:  
    Cannot be established without clinical information.  

  - Red flags considered:  
    None identified due to absence of reported symptoms or history.

P:  
  - Disposition:  
    Recommend patient call back or seek in-person evaluation if symptoms develop or worsen.  

  - Plan:  
    No treatment can be recommended without clinical information. Advise patient to monitor for any new or worsening symptoms.  

  - Safety net:  
    Instruct patient to seek urgent care or emergency services if experiencing any of the following: severe pain, difficulty breathing, chest pain, sudden weakness or numbness, high fever, or any other concerning symptoms. Follow up with primary care provider as needed.
patient_instructions: If you are not improving as expected, or if symptoms worsen, seek in-person medical care.
Medication options you may consider if safe (no dosing by phone): acetaminophen (Tylenol) if you can take it safely; ibuprofen (Advil) if you can take it safely.
